steps.title: Find the slope from two points
steps.content:
The graph passes through the points $(-2, 0)$ and $(0, -4)$. Using the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{-4 - 0}{0 - (-2)} = \frac{-4}{2} = -2$$

steps.title: Write the equation for the line shown
steps.content:
The line has slope $-2$ and y-intercept $-4$ (since it passes through $(0, -4)$). The equation of the line is:
$$y = f(x) - 11 = -2x - 4$$

steps.title: Add 11 to both sides to find f(x)
